explanations.9: The regex /\/blog\/tags\/([^/]+)/ extracts the tag name from a URL path like '/blog/tags/javascript' by capturing everything after 'tags/' until the next slash.
explanations.10: decodeURIComponent converts URL-encoded characters (like %20 for spaces) back to normal text so 'my%20tag' becomes 'my tag'.
explanations.31: This creates a unique list of all tags across all posts by flattening the tags arrays and removing duplicates using a Set.
explanations.32: This finds the original tag name by comparing it against slug versions—so if 'JavaScript' was slugified to 'javascript', we find and display the original 'JavaScript'.
explanations.37: The optional chaining operator ?. safely checks if post.tags exists before calling some(), preventing errors if a post has no tags.
